title: #[non_exhaustive] on all spec enums prevents silent match exhaustion on spec additions
description: Every enum that mirrors a spec-defined set (ChatKind, DeliveryState, ContactPresence, OwnerPresence, ChatMemberRole, ChannelPermissionTargetType, SpanType, PushUrgency, QuotaScope, ChatStreamDataType) is marked #[non_exhaustive]. This is correct because the spec is a draft and WILL add new values. Without #[non_exhaustive], adding a new spec variant is a breaking change: any downstream crate with a match on these enums would stop compiling. A contributor might argue #[non_exhaustive] is redundant since an Unknown catch-all already handles runtime unknowns. That conflates two separate guarantees: Unknown handles wire values not recognized at runtime; #[non_exhaustive] enforces at compile time that external match arms include a wildcard, making future enum growth non-breaking at the API level.
